what_it_does: This Vitest test file checks that documentation and source files in the recollection project explicitly mention both Claude Code and Codex invocation paths and describe episodic memory as cross-harness rather than Claude-only.
observations: Defines REPO_ROOT from import.meta.dirname and a read(relPath) helper that reads UTF-8 files relative to the repo root.
First test loads skills/remembering-conversations/SKILL.md and asserts it contains 'Claude Code', 'Codex', 'Task tool', and 'MCP tools directly'.
Second test loads multiple files and asserts they contain 'Claude Code and Codex conversations'; README.md is additionally checked for 'Codex plugin' and '~/.codex/sessions'.
No mocks, no external test fixtures, no setup/teardown.
standards_out: Acts as a documentation/source-consistency guard for multi-harness (Claude Code + Codex) support.
Expects README.md to reference a Codex plugin and the ~/.codex/sessions directory.
conventions: Shared helper function for file reading.
concerns: String-inclusion assertions are brittle and will break if wording changes.
readFileSync will throw if any referenced file is missing, with no explicit error handling.
Tests share the same read helper and file-system state with no isolation.
notes: A cross-harness documentation guard test that makes sure the project speaks to both Claude Code and Codex users.
questions: What specific invocation or UI does 'Task tool' refer to in SKILL.md?
Is '~/.codex/sessions' a canonical or expected Codex session directory?
Why is src/mcp-server.ts expected to contain the marketing-style phrase 'Claude Code and Codex conversations' rather than an implementation detail?
purpose_vs_actual: The describe block's title 'Codex-aware skills' matches the tests, which verify cross-harness references across skills, prompts, agents, source, and README.
apparent_themes: Consistency across skills, prompts, agents, source, and README
intention_signals: The file tries to enforce that the recollection project is not Claude-Code-only and explicitly documents Codex as a first-class harness.
novelty: It codifies awareness of a parallel AI coding tool (Codex) as a test requirement rather than treating it as an afterthought.
contribution: Provides an automated check that project documentation and source remain accurate for both Claude Code and Codex contexts, reducing drift in multi-harness messaging.
summary_for_embedding: Vitest test in company/recollection/test/codex-skills.test.ts that asserts documentation and source files mention both Claude Code and Codex and describe episodic memory as cross-harness.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to keep the recollection project's skills, prompts, agents, and README aligned with a cross-harness (Claude Code + Codex) narrative.

symbols.does: Reads a file relative to the repository root and returns its UTF-8 contents.
symbols.description: A small helper that joins REPO_ROOT with the provided relPath and returns the file contents via readFileSync. The tests use it to load documentation and source files for string-inclusion assertions.
